<pr_description>
Consider the following PR description:
Use proper whitespace in CREATE INDEX statements
Description
	 
		(last modified by Hannes Ljungberg)
	 
Creating an index through:
index = Index(
	fields=['-name’],
	name='idx'
)
Will generate the valid but not so pretty CREATE INDEX statement: 
CREATE INDEX "idx" ON "schema_author" ("name"DESC)
The following would be expected:
CREATE INDEX "idx" ON "schema_author" ("name" DESC)
This was partially fixed for indexes using opclasses in https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/30903#ticket but it introduced a new quirk when opclasses is used without explicit ordering:
index = Index(
	fields=['name’],
	name='idx'
	opclasses=['text_pattern_ops’]
)
Will result in:
CREATE INDEX "idx" ON "schema_author" (“name” text_pattern_ops )
Note the whitespace after text_pattern_ops. When used with a descending order it will look correct. 
Unfortunately in the fix in #30903 it was assumed that the col_suffixes passed to django.db.backends.ddl_references.Columns would be empty for ascending order but instead it will contain empty strings and thus causing this bug. See: ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/db/backends/ddl_references.py#L87
The expected output would be:
CREATE INDEX "idx" ON "schema_author" (“name” text_pattern_ops)

</pr_description>

Now I understand the issue clearly. Let me analyze the two problems:

1. **`Columns.__str__` (line 87)**: When a column has a suffix like `'DESC'`, it produces `"name"DESC` without a space before the suffix. It should be `"name" DESC`.

2. **`IndexColumns.__str__` (line 112-120)**: When opclasses are used without ordering (suffix is empty string `''`), it produces `"name" text_pattern_ops ` (with trailing space). The issue is that it always adds a space before the col_suffix, even when the suffix is empty.

Let me look at how `col_suffixes` are generated:

In `indexes.py` line 51: `col_suffixes = [order[1] for order in self.fields_orders]`

And `fields_orders` (line 31-34) creates tuples like `(field_name, 'DESC')` for descending or `(field_name, '')` for ascending.

So `col_suffixes` will be `['DESC']` or `['']` (empty string).

Now let me fix both issues:

**Fix 1 - `Columns.__str__`**: Add a space before the suffix when it's non-empty.

**Fix 2 - `IndexColumns.__str__`**: Only add the suffix with a space when it's non-empty.
